name: Potato Salad with Bratwursts
recipeIngredient: 2 Bratwursts (precooked or fresh pork, veal, or turkey)
2 medium Spanish onions, cut into 1/2-inch dice
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the potatoes in a 3-quart pot. Cover with water and add salt and tarragon.
2. Add fresh bratwursts (if using) and bring to boil.
3. Boil, uncovered, until potatoes are almost tender but still have some firmness.
4. Add onions, peas, and carrots. Cook for 1 minute longer.
5. Drain contents of pot into a large, fine strainer.
6. When potatoes are cool enough to handle, cut them in half.
7. Split the cooked or precooked brats lengthwise, then into 1/2-inch thick slices.
8. Heat oil in a 10-inch non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
9. Add sliced brats and cook until well browned (about 3 minutes), stirring often.
10. Use a slotted spoon to transfer the brats to a 2-quart mixing bowl.
11. Add potatoes, cut side down, to the skillet. Cook until browned (about 2 minutes).
12. Use a slotted spoon to transfer the potatoes and sliced green onions and remaining vegetables from the strainer to the bowl.
13. Gently toss to combine.
14. For dressing: Add all ingredients to any fat remaining in the skillet. Stir well.
15. Heat through but do not boil, then pour over the salad.
16. Toss gently to combine and adjust seasoning.
17. The dish can be served immediately or refrigerated for up to 1 day. If refrigerated, toss gently to mix ingredients and adjust seasoning.
18. To serve, arrange lettuce leaves on a serving plate. Mound the potato salad on top. Alternatively, the salad can be served in shallow soup plates (omit lettuce).
